title: Original adaptation of a SaaS dashboard and application shell
productContext: A fictional B2B SaaS company (product name 'Paneflow') is adapting a publicly accessible marketing-and-product surface at https://example.com/c2-saas-pilot/ into an original SaaS dashboard and application shell. The bound design-source snapshot captured the marketing routes only; the authenticated product dashboard itself was NOT inspected (the crawl was unauthenticated) and MUST NOT be claimed as observed. Two specific migration hazards are in scope: (1) RESPONSIVE DISAGREEMENT — the source site's desktop and mobile layouts disagree on navigation order, primary-action placement, or content priority, and the adaptation must resolve the disagreement explicitly rather than silently picking one; (2) CONSENT BANNERS — the source site surfaces a cookie / consent banner whose treatment must be adapted generically without copying the source vendor's branded copy or pre-ticked preferences. Every URL in this brief is synthetic and uses example.com.
users: SaaS admin configuring the dashboard shell after migration
end-user navigating the migrated marketing surface and the dashboard entry
jobs: perceive a consistent navigation order and primary-action placement across desktop and mobile after the responsive disagreement is resolved
encounter a consent banner whose treatment is generic and not copied from the source vendor, with no pre-ticked preferences
reach the dashboard entry from the migrated marketing surface without crossing into a claimed-but-uninspected authenticated route
requiredJourneys: admin opens the migrated dashboard shell on desktop and mobile and perceives a consistent, explicitly-resolved navigation and primary-action treatment
end-user encounters the adapted consent banner, can reject all non-essential cookies as easily as accepting, and is not shown pre-ticked preferences
end-user reaches the dashboard entry from the marketing surface and the boundary between inspected-marketing and not-inspected-authenticated is clearly marked
constraints: The adaptation MUST be ORIGINAL: it must not reproduce the source site's proprietary copy, wordmark, exact palette, unique illustrations, distinctive iconography, OR the source consent-banner vendor's branded copy or pre-ticked preference defaults.

The responsive disagreement (desktop and mobile navigation order / primary-action placement / content priority) MUST be resolved explicitly with a recorded decision; silently cloning the desktop or mobile variant as-is is forbidden.

The consent banner MUST be adapted generically with 'reject all non-essential' as easy as 'accept all'; pre-ticked preferences, consent walls that block all access, or copy that imitates the source consent vendor are forbidden.
Only the routes marked 'inspected' in the bound snapshot (marketing surface) may ground a claim; the authenticated dashboard route was NOT inspected and no claim may rely on having observed it.
Every claim about the source site must trace to an inspected route in the snapshot; speculative dashboard widgets, invented metrics, or fabricated feature lists are forbidden.
The product name Paneflow is fictional; do not imply affiliation with any real SaaS product, dashboard framework, or consent-management vendor observed or implied by the source.

mobileRules: the resolved navigation order must be consistent with the desktop order after the responsive-disagreement decision, with the change made explicit in code or comments
the primary action remains reachable above the fold on mobile without horizontal scroll after relocation
the 'reject all non-essential' control must be at least as prominent and tappable as the 'accept all' control in a single-handed portrait grip
expanded preferences must not pre-tick any non-essential category
